_comment: Known local LLM runtime catalog. Maintained by Rebecca's landscape scan. Do not edit manually — use the update process.
_update_task: Queue Rebecca with goal: research local LLM runtimes landscape as of today

runtimes.label: LM Studio
runtimes.description: Desktop GUI for running local LLMs. Easy model download, built-in chat, OpenAI-compatible server.
runtimes.amd_rocm_support: partial — depends on llama.cpp backend, some GPU detection issues on Linux
warnings: Malware scare reported March 2026 — download only from official lmstudio.ai
runtimes.notes: Best for beginners. GUI-first, server mode optional. Security incident March 2026 (v0.4.7+4 malware report — verify downloads).

runtimes.label: Ollama
runtimes.description: One-command model runner. Pull and serve models via CLI. OpenAI-compatible API.
runtimes.amd_rocm_support: good — via ROCm Docker images; some unsupported older GPUs need workarounds
runtimes.notes: Best for quick setup. Huge model library. AMD users: use official ROCm Docker image.

runtimes.label: vLLM
runtimes.description: High-throughput GPU inference server. Supports PagedAttention, continuous batching. Production-grade.
runtimes.amd_rocm_support: good — ROCm install: pip install vllm --extra-index-url https://download.pytorch.org/whl/rocm6.2
new_features: vLLM V1 core architecture rewrite (chunked prefill, zero-overhead scheduling)
runtimes.notes: Best for large GPU servers. Can serve non-text models (speech, vision, audio) — always check modality of loaded model. Auth-protected endpoints common in production.

runtimes.label: SGLang
runtimes.description: Structured generation language + fast runtime. RadixAttention for KV cache reuse. Strong for long-context.
runtimes.amd_rocm_support: growing — community reports, check GitHub for latest ROCm status
new_features: MLA (Multi-head Latent Attention) for DeepSeek models
runtimes.notes: Rapidly growing. Often cited as faster than vLLM for multi-turn conversations. Strong for long-context.

runtimes.label: LMDeploy
runtimes.description: InternLM team's inference framework. TurboMind engine, fast on NVIDIA. Good quantization support.
warnings: CVE-2026-33626 — critical vulnerability exploited in the wild; run latest patched version (v0.12.3+) and do not expose to public internet
runtimes.notes: Strong quantization toolchain. TurboMind engine often faster than vLLM for single-GPU NVIDIA. NVIDIA-only for production use.

runtimes.label: TabbyAPI
runtimes.description: Lightweight OpenAI-compatible server focused on GPTQ/EXL2 quantized models.
runtimes.notes: Best for EXL2 format models. Lower VRAM usage than vLLM for same quality. NVIDIA-only.

runtimes.label: Unsloth Studio
runtimes.description: Fine-tuning studio with inference. Optimized kernels for training and serving. GUI included.
runtimes.amd_rocm_support: partial — fine-tuning primary use case; inference secondary
runtimes.notes: Primary use: fine-tuning. Can serve fine-tuned models. Not a general-purpose inference server.

runtimes.label: llama.cpp server
runtimes.description: C++ inference engine. Runs on CPU, GPU, Apple Silicon. GGUF format. Widest hardware support.
runtimes.amd_rocm_support: good — via Vulkan backend; also ROCm/HIP; Strix Halo MTP benchmarks published
new_features: MTP (Multi-Token Prediction) support — significant speed gain for supported models (27B much faster on Strix Halo)
tensor offloading optimization: offload tensors not layers for 200%+ speed improvement
runtimes.notes: Widest hardware support. Q4_K_M recommended for quality/speed balance. Repo moved to ggml-org. MTP is significant speed gain for supported models.

runtimes.label: KoboldCpp
runtimes.description: llama.cpp wrapper with storytelling UI. Good for creative tasks and offline use.
runtimes.notes: Niche: creative writing and roleplay focus. Not recommended for agent tasks.

runtimes.label: Lemonade Server
runtimes.description: AMD-first omni-modal inference server. Auto-optimizes for AMD NPU (Ryzen AI) and GPU. Text, vision, audio.
runtimes.amd_rocm_support: excellent — specifically targets AMD NPU (Ryzen AI) and GPU; auto-optimizes
runtimes.notes: NEW in 2025/2026. Omni-modal (text, images, speech). Automatically optimizes for your GPU and NPU. Open source. Best choice for AMD Strix Halo / Ryzen AI Max users. Add to doctor detection.

free_external_apis.label: OpenRouter
free_external_apis.notes: Largest model selection. Free tier available. Routes to many providers.

free_external_apis.label: Groq
free_external_apis.notes: Very fast inference (LPU hardware). Free tier with rate limits. Good for quick tasks.

free_external_apis.label: Cerebras
free_external_apis.notes: Generous free tier as of May 2026. Fast inference. Good fallback brain option.

free_external_apis.label: Together AI
free_external_apis.notes: Wide model selection including open-weight models. Free credits on signup.

free_external_apis.label: GitHub Models
free_external_apis.notes: Free via GitHub account. Limited but useful for bootstrapping.

modality_notes: Modern models are multimodal — a single endpoint may serve text+vision+thinking+tool-calling+audio+embedding. The 'supported_modalities' field per runtime describes what the runtime CAN serve, not what any specific loaded model supports. Auth-protected endpoints (e.g. vLLM in production) should be flagged as 'modality unknown' rather than assumed to be text-only.
needs_research: Lemonade Server default port — not documented in May 2026 scan

FlashAttention-3 adoption status across runtimes beyond vLLM
Quantization format comparison: GGUF Q4_K_M vs EXL2 vs AWQ vs GPTQ quality/speed tradeoffs
